attribution.rights_note: Imported llmwiki-style corpus requires review before promotion.

review_guidance.overview: Review concepts first, then inspect representative claims and their source observations before promotion.
priorities: Focus reviewer effort on concepts with strong grounded claims and explicit citations first.
Downgrade or reject concepts whose claims are fragmented, duplicated, or missing meaningful support.
For academic material, citation-bearing claims deserve special scrutiny for fit, contradiction, and fabrication risk.
citation_guidance: A citation key or extracted reference is evidence of traceability, not correctness.
Check whether the cited work actually supports the claim and whether the claim overstates it.
Use the citation track to prioritize claims that can move into a separate citation-ingestion workflow.

options.label: Trusted
options.help: Promote this concept and its supported claims when the evidence and wording are ready.

options.label: Provisional
options.help: Keep this concept in reviewed state when it is promising but still needs citation or wording cleanup.

options.label: Needs Review
options.help: Leave undecided when support, scope, or concept boundaries are still unclear.

options.label: Rejected
options.help: Exclude this concept when it is noise, unsupported, duplicated, or misleading.

field_specs.label: Concept description
field_specs.help: Refine the concept summary to match the strongest supported interpretation.

field_specs.label: Reviewer notes
field_specs.help: Record why this concept is trusted, provisional, rejected, or still unclear.

field_specs.label: Prerequisites
field_specs.help: List prerequisite concepts only when the manuscript support is explicit or defensible.

options.label: Unreviewed
options.help: Keep this citation candidate in triage until fit and existence are checked.

options.label: Verified
options.help: The cited work exists and materially supports the associated manuscript claim.

options.label: Needs Source Check
options.help: The citation may be useful but still needs direct source inspection or metadata cleanup.

options.label: Misleading
options.help: The citation exists but overstates, contradicts, or poorly fits the claim.

options.label: Irrelevant
options.help: The citation does not materially support the concept or claim under review.

options.label: Fabricated
options.help: The citation appears invented, malformed, or otherwise not real.

citation_field_specs.label: Citation notes
citation_field_specs.help: Record whether the cited work exists, fits the claim, or should move into a dedicated citation-ingestion lane.

concept_reviews.title: Lecture 1. Example
concept_reviews.description: Imported from doclift bundle document kind 'lecture'.
concept_reviews.review_help: Prefer `trusted` when claims are coherent and citation-bearing support is appropriate; prefer `provisional` when the concept is plausible but still needs citation or wording cleanup.

next_actions: Promote citation-bearing claims into a dedicated citation review lane.
Use CiteGeist extraction as a first pass, then verify support and metadata before trusting the citation.
